Home / Alternatives to Asana / Asana vs OpenProject

Asana vs OpenProject

A side-by-side look at Asana (the paid SaaS) and OpenProject (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Asana OpenProject
Tagline Project and task management for teams. Enterprise-ready open source project management.
License Proprietary SaaS GPL-3.0
Pricing Free tier for up to 10; Premium from $10.99/user/month. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 3/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Teams that need Gantt, roadmaps and enterprise controls.

OpenProject strengths

  • Strong Gantt, roadmaps and budget features.
  • BIM (Building Information Modeling) for construction industry.
  • Active enterprise adoption.

OpenProject weaknesses

  • Community edition lacks some premium features.
  • Setup has more moving parts than lightweight tools.
  • UI density can intimidate newcomers.

What's the catch with Asana?

  • Pricing jumps significantly beyond Basic tier.
  • Steep learning curve for advanced features.
  • {'Lock-in': 'exporting workflows is painful.'}

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Asana: see Asana alternatives, or learn more about OpenProject on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
